name: Cauliflower Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the cauliflower in boiling, salted water until tender. Drain well.
2. Mash the cauliflower.
3. Melt butter in a medium saucepan. Blend in flour and cook, stirring over medium heat until bubbly. Do not let the mixture brown.
4. Meanwhile, bring milk to a boil in another saucepan.
5. Add hot milk to the butter-flour mixture and cook, stirring over medium heat until the sauce thickens and is smooth. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste.
6. Remove from heat.
7. Lightly beat egg yolks and stir into the sauce. Stir in cheese and mashed cauliflower until smooth.
8. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
9. Gently fold the cauliflower mixture into the beaten egg whites.
10. Pour the batter into a buttered 2-quart (2 L) souffle dish.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 30 to 40 minutes, or until puffed and firm, lightly browned.
12. Serve immediately.
SERVES: 4-6
